  ResearchArea: 1. **Solid State Chemistry** - Investigation of Novel Oxides Synthesis, characterization, and development of structure-property relationships in new functional inorganic materials, 2. **Energy Storage Materials -** Synthesis, and Electrochemical study Alkali metal-ion battery systems, 3. **Investigating Electrode Materials in Real-time -** Employing state of art techniques to study electrode materials in real-time (in-situ)

Summary: The Community 0 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Anil Kumar Paidi. This group focuses on three primary research areas: Solid State Chemistry, Energy Storage Materials, and Investigating Electrode Materials in Real-time. They are connected through their collaborative work, with Anil Kumar Paidi being the common thread among them, as indicated by 6 relationships (RESEARCHES_IN).

reports.text:
Community 2 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Avinash Raulo
  ResearchArea: Energy Storage (Li-S, Li-ion, Li-metal and other rechargeable batteries, Supercapacitors), Material (Soft materials and Polymers, Inorganic and Carbon Materials, Conductive Composites, Biomass) for Energy Application
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 8

Summary: The Community 2 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty member Avinash Raulo. This group focuses on energy storage and material research for energy applications, specifically exploring rechargeable batteries (Li-S, Li-ion, Li-metal) and supercapacitors, as well as soft materials, polymers, inorganic and carbon materials, and conductive composites derived from biomass.

members: idp:Our lab at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues
idp:particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers. This research emphasizes the significance of both coding and noncoding regulatory elements in the genome (REGULOME)
idp:which drive cellular heterogeneity and plasticity. By employing advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models

idp:we aim to map these regulatory elements to uncover mechanisms underlying disease progression and treatment resistance. The ultimate goal is to develop targeted therapies that address tissue heterogeneity and improve women's reproductive health

reports.text:
Community 4 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Santhilal Subhash
  ResearchArea: Our lab at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues, particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers. This research emphasizes the significance of both coding and noncoding regulatory elements in the genome (REGULOME), which drive cellular heterogeneity and plasticity. By employing advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models, we aim to map these regulatory elements to uncover mechanisms underlying disease progression and treatment resistance. The ultimate goal is to develop targeted therapies that address tissue heterogeneity and improve women's reproductive health, Key Focus Areas, Reproductive Biology, Genomics & Single Cell Biology, Multi-omics, Bioinformatics
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 9

Summary: The Community 4 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Santhilal Subhash. This group focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues through research on genomics, single-cell biology, multi-omics, and bioinformatics, with a goal of developing targeted therapies for gynecological diseases and cancers. They employ advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models to achieve this objective.
reports.summary: The Community 4 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Santhilal Subhash. This group focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues through research on genomics, single-cell biology, multi-omics, and bioinformatics, with a goal of developing targeted therapies for gynecological diseases and cancers. They employ advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models to achieve this objective.

members: idp:Genome Analysis - We are embarking on an innovative venture to develop energy-efficient genome analysis solutions through hardware/software co-design

idp:specifically targeting low-power and affordable platforms. The focus is on genome assembly and downstream analysis using data generated from 2nd and 3rd generation sequencing technology. In additions
idp:we intent to develop affordable hardware-based metagenomic and anti-microbial resistance monitoring pipelines

idp:Battery Management System - Improving the battery management system (BMS) in electric vehicles (EVs) through the integration of machine learning (ML) via hardware/software co-design at the edge holds great potential for enhancing the overall performance of EVs. By leveraging ML in the BMS
idp:several key areas can be targeted to achieve longer battery life

reports.text:
Community 5 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Sidharth Maheshwari
  ResearchArea: Genome Analysis, Hardware-Software Co-Design, Battery Management Systems, Machine Learning (Tsetlin Machines), Genome Analysis - We are embarking on an innovative venture to develop energy-efficient genome analysis solutions through hardware/software co-design, specifically targeting low-power and affordable platforms. The focus is on genome assembly and downstream analysis using data generated from 2nd and 3rd generation sequencing technology. In additions, we intent to develop affordable hardware-based metagenomic and anti-microbial resistance monitoring pipelines, Battery Management System - Improving the battery management system (BMS) in electric vehicles (EVs) through the integration of machine learning (ML) via hardware/software co-design at the edge holds great potential for enhancing the overall performance of EVs. By leveraging ML in the BMS, several key areas can be targeted to achieve longer battery life, efficient charge capacity utilization
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 10

Summary: The Community 5 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Sidharth Maheshwari. This group focuses on innovative research areas including Genome Analysis, Hardware-Software Co-Design, Battery Management Systems, and Machine Learning (specifically Tsetlin Machines). They aim to develop energy-efficient solutions for genome analysis and improve battery management in electric vehicles through the integration of machine learning via hardware/software co-design. The group has a significant body of research with 10 papers listed under Sidharth Maheshwari's name.
